text.title: Gumroad Ops Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Gumroad listing must satisfy. Hard rules; designed to prevent cover-blur, MoR confusion, broken affiliate flows, and unattributed listings.

rule.statement: Every listing carries 6 mandatory fields: cover_url (≥1280×720), subtitle (≤280 chars), price (fixed | pwyw | tiers), product_type (file | license | link), refund_policy (one of Gumroad's 4 enums), tags (1-5 from Gumroad's allowlist).
rule.rationale: Listings missing cover, subtitle, or tags convert <30% of the median; the six-field minimum is empirically the conversion floor.

rule.statement: Gumroad acts as Merchant of Record. You do NOT collect VAT/GST yourself on Gumroad-billed transactions. Your invoicing system must NOT issue a separate VAT invoice to the buyer for those sales.

rationale.source: Gumroad MoR terms; EU VAT framework

rule.rationale: Double-taxing the buyer is the most expensive Gumroad mistake — leads to chargebacks and account suspension. Gumroad remits VAT to the relevant tax authority on your behalf and provides MoR receipts.

rule.statement: For software / license-keyed products, license key delivery uses Gumroad's License API (`/v2/licenses/verify`). Each download includes a unique key; your client validates at startup and on a daily cadence.

rationale.source: Gumroad License API docs; SaaS piracy mitigation patterns

rule.rationale: Hard-coded keys in the download bundle defeat the point. License API also lets you revoke on refund without rebuilding the binary.

rule.statement: Affiliate commission is set between 30% and 50% for indie launches. Below 30% does not motivate; above 50% leaves the founder margin-thin on Gumroad's 10% + payment fees.
rule.rationale: 30% is the floor where affiliates promote; 50% is the ceiling where founder still nets positive after platform + payment fees.

rule.statement: Gumroad copy A/B experiments run minimum 14 days OR until 100 conversions per variant — whichever comes first. Variants flipped earlier are inconclusive and burn the test slot.

rationale.source: A/B-test discipline; Gumroad seller-forum experiment threads

rule.rationale: Most indie products see <50 sales/wk; below this threshold, two weeks is the minimum window for any kind of signal.

rule.statement: Monthly: pull Gumroad dashboard CSV. For each listing, compute conversion rate, refund rate, affiliate share, and rank by 30-day revenue. Promote the top 2 to feature spots (Gumroad Discover, your own site hero); demote the bottom 2 (archive or unbundle).
rule.rationale: Without monthly pruning, the catalogue rots and dashboard noise hides the few products that actually compound.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the 6-step procedure end-to-end and run the validator before publishing the listing.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree run leaf for canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree skip leaf so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out.
